title: Hot, uncomfortable weather expected on Wednesday
description: GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - Very hot and...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - Very hot and uncomfortable conditions are expected over the interior of Namakwa in the Northern Cape, West Coast and Central Karoo districts, including Breede Valley and Oudtshoorn municipalities tomorrow, Wednesday 12 November.


> Day time temperatures are expected to reach the mid to high 30s.


These conditions may persist over the West Coast until Friday, but updates will be issued accordingly.

Such weather conditions can have significant health impacts, so it is essential to take necessary precautions to protect yourself. The most serious health and safety concern is heat stroke. Heat stroke can be fatal if medical attention is not available immediately.


## Extended weather forecast for Wednesday and Thursday, 12-13 November:


Fine in the western interior, otherwise partly cloudy and warm to hot with isolated showers and thundershowers in the east.

Western CapeMorning fog patches along the west coast as well as the south coast where it will be partly cloudy and cool, otherwise fine and warm to hot but very hot in places. The wind along the coast will be light and variable along the south coast becoming moderate south-westerly to westerly from the afternoon, otherwise moderate to fresh south-easterly to southerly.

The expected UVB sunburn index: Very High

Western half of the Eastern CapeMorning fog patches in places over the interior, otherwise fine and warm to hot. The wind along the coast will be moderate south-westerly, becoming fresh to strong.

Eastern half of the Eastern CapeMorning fog patches in places over the interior, otherwise fine and warm to hot, becoming partly cloudy with isolated thundershowers in the extreme eastern interior. The wind along the coast will be moderate south-westerly.


> Forecast on Thursday 13 November:


There will be light rain along the south and south-east on Thursday.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
